mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency falls below 55% in low-rate environments.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.5 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the t-statistic for the return differential is insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in both series fail to predict positive returns with > 55% accuracy over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side momentum that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC < 0.01 during monetary tightening phases or when annual inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with persistently low federal funds rates creates expectations of imminent monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of cases when both conditions are met, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion that supports household income growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll surges fail to predict positive returns with > 50% accuracy over a 10-year rolling window, or the signal reverses direction during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index indicate contracting manufacturing activity that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive declines in industrial production are followed by positive returns in > 60% of historical instances, or the predictive IC is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low federal funds rates below 0.5% stimulate mortgage demand and housing market activity, which amplifies consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts and low rates fail to predict positive equity returns with > 55% accuracy, or the mechanism breaks down when housing inventory exceeds 6 months of supply.
